load_file.description: Loads the specified file and returns a string with the dataset name associated with the file. Supports loading specific geometry indices and optional field-on-demand mode.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the file to load.

parameters.name: -alldata
parameters.desc: Optional. Loads all data from the file regardless of the user preference settings.

parameters.name: -fod
parameters.desc: Optional. Loads field data on demand, reducing initial memory usage.

parameters.name: -geoms
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the geometry indices to load. Example: {0 2}.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies a custom dataset name.

parameters.name: -parts
parameters.desc: Optional. Displays and controls region parts independently if they exist.

unload_file.description: Unloads all the datasets belonging to the specified file, freeing associated memory.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the file to unload.

reload_files.description: Reloads the specified files from disk, updating all associated datasets.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of file names to reload.

parameters.name: -alldata
parameters.desc: Optional. Reloads all data from the files regardless of the user preference settings.

reload_datasets.description: Reloads all the specified datasets from their source files, refreshing the displayed data.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of dataset names to reload.

parameters.name: -alldata
parameters.desc: Optional. Reloads all data from the files regardless of the user preference settings.

list_files.description: Returns a list of opened files according to the given pattern. If no pattern is specified, all files are returned. Can filter to files contained in the selected plot.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern to filter file names.

parameters.name: -fullpath
parameters.desc: Optional. Returns the full file path instead of just the file name.

parameters.name: -selected
parameters.desc: Optional. Restricts the list to files contained in the selected plot only.

list_datasets.description: Returns a list of dataset names according to the given pattern. Can filter by dimension, a specific plot, or the selected plot.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern to filter dataset names.

parameters.name: -dim
parameters.desc: Optional. Dimension of datasets to filter: 1, 2, or 3.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Filters datasets by plot name, or use -selected for the selected plot.

remove_datasets.description: Removes the specified datasets from the current session, freeing associated memory and visualizations.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of dataset names to remove.

load_file_datasets.description: Loads datasets from the specified file and returns a list of dataset names. Similar to load_file but returns a list instead of a single string.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the file to load datasets from.

parameters.name: -alldata
parameters.desc: Optional. Loads all data from the file regardless of the user preference settings.

parameters.name: -geoms
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the geometry indices to load. Example: {0 2}.

parameters.name: -parts
parameters.desc: Optional. Displays and controls region parts independently if they exist.

load_script_file.description: Loads and executes a Tcl script or Inspect script. The -inspect flag forces execution as an Inspect script.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the Tcl script file to load and execute.

parameters.name: -inspect
parameters.desc: Optional. Forces Sentaurus Visual to execute the script as an Inspect script instead of a Tcl script.

load_library.description: Loads a Sentaurus Visual script library. Can load all libraries from default paths, all libraries from a specified path, or a single named library.

parameters.name: -all
parameters.desc: Loads all libraries located at the default library paths.

parameters.name: -path
parameters.desc: Path to the directory containing libraries. An optional second argument specifies a particular library name.

get_input_data.description: Displays a customizable dialog box that requires user input. Works only in interactive mode unless a default value is provided.

parameters.name: -default
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the default value returned if the user cancels the dialog.

parameters.name: -desc
parameters.desc: Optional. Descriptive text displayed in the dialog box to prompt the user.

parameters.name: -double | -file | -int | -list | -string | -yesno
parameters.desc: Optional. Input type. "double" expects a floating-point number, "file" opens a file dialog, "int" expects an integer, "list" selects from a dropdown, "string" expects free-form text (default), "yesno" returns 1 for Yes and 0 for No.

parameters.name: -elements
parameters.desc: Optional. List of selectable elements for list-type input.

get_input_data.example: get_input_data -desc "Variable that defines the cut point." -default 0.0 -double

list_tdr_states.description: Returns a list of state names for the geometry visualized in a plot. States represent different time steps or bias points stored in TDR files.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern that the state name must match. If not specified, all state names are returned.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot containing the geometry. Defaults to the selected plot.

import_settings.description: Imports Sentaurus Visual settings from a previously saved configuration file, restoring visual preferences and layout.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Path to the configuration file (.conf) to import.

export_settings.description: Exports the current Sentaurus Visual settings to a configuration file for later reuse or sharing.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the output configuration file. Must have the .conf file extension.

reset_settings.description: Resets all Sentaurus Visual settings to their default factory values.

set_best_look.description: Automatically configures various plot parameters to produce a visually optimized layout, including axis labels, colors, and scales.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: Optional. List of plot names to apply best look settings to. If not specified, applies to the selected plot.

render_mode.description: Controls whether plots are rendered during loading. If rendering is switched off during batch operations, it must be switched back on to display the loaded plots. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -on | -off
parameters.desc: Optional. "-on" enables rendering during plot loading, "-off" disables it for faster batch processing. If omitted, returns the current rendering status.

set_window_size.description: Sets the size of the main Sentaurus Visual window in pixels. Minimum size is 200x200.

parameters.name: <width>x<height>
parameters.desc: Window dimensions in pixels, specified as width followed by x followed by height. Example: 1280x800.

set_window_full.description: Toggles the full plot view mode, which maximizes the plot area within the main window.

parameters.name: -on | -off
parameters.desc: "-on" activates full plot view, "-off" deactivates it.

windows_style.signature: windows_style [-aspect_ratio_on | -aspect_ratio_off] [-direction right_down | down_right] [-max <intValue>] [-sort <stringList>] [-style horizontal | vertical | grid | max | custom]
windows_style.description: Specifies the layout style for arranging multiple plots in the Sentaurus Visual window, including orientation, aspect ratio, and grid configuration.

parameters.name: -aspect_ratio_on | -aspect_ratio_off
parameters.desc: Optional. "-aspect_ratio_on" maintains the aspect ratio for all plots, "-aspect_ratio_off" allows independent resizing.

parameters.name: -direction
parameters.desc: Optional. "right_down" fills the grid to the right first then continues downward; "down_right" fills downward first then continues to the right.

parameters.name: -max
parameters.desc: Optional. Maximum number of columns when using custom grid style.

parameters.name: -sort
parameters.desc: Optional. List of plot names specifying the display order.

parameters.name: -style
parameters.desc: Optional. Layout style: "horizontal" (single row), "vertical" (single column), "grid" (auto-arranged grid), "max" (maximize available space), "custom" (user-defined grid with -max).

set_transformation.description: Applies a geometric transformation (scaling, shifting, or both) to a structure in a 2D or 3D plot. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -scale
parameters.desc: Scale factors for each axis as {x y z}, where values are positive doubles. Example: {0.5 1 1} halves the x-axis.

parameters.name: -shift
parameters.desc: Shift offsets for each axis as {x y z} in coordinate units. Example: {1.0 0 0} shifts the structure by 1.0 along x.

parameters.name: -geom
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the geometry to transform. Defaults to the main geometry of the active plot.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot containing the geometry. Defaults to the selected plot.

set_deformation.description: Sets the deformation properties for a structure by applying a vector field, or creates a new plot with the deformed structure. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the vector field to use for deforming the structure.

parameters.name: -factor
parameters.desc: Optional. Multiplication factor applied to the deformation vector. Must be greater than zero. Default: 1.

parameters.name: -reset
parameters.desc: Optional. Resets the current deformation to the undeformed state.

parameters.name: -new_plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Creates a new plot with the deformed structure instead of modifying the existing plot.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to deform. Defaults to the selected plot.

version.description: Returns the version string of the running Sentaurus Visual instance.

help.description: Displays information about Sentaurus Visual Tcl commands. Without arguments, returns a complete list of available commands. With a command name, returns its syntax.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of a Sentaurus Visual Tcl command to query.

echo.description: Prints a string to the Sentaurus Visual console output.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Text to be printed in the console.

show_msg.description: Displays a message in a dialog box with configurable severity level and title.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Text to be displayed in the message dialog.

parameters.name: -error | -info | -warning
parameters.desc: Optional. Severity level: "-error" (error icon), "-info" (information icon, default), "-warning" (warning icon).

parameters.name: -title
parameters.desc: Optional. Title of the dialog box window.

show_msg.example: show_msg -warning -title "Bad Value" "There was a problem extracting the threshold voltage"

exit.description: Exits Sentaurus Visual with the given status code.

parameters.name: <intValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Exit status code as an integer. Default: 0.

undo.description: Undoes the last command or the specified number of recently executed commands.

parameters.name: <intValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Number of commands to revert. If not specified, undoes the last command only.

start_movie.description: Starts recording a new movie by creating a new frame buffer. Use add_frame to capture individual frames and export_movie to save the result.

parameters.name: -resolution
parameters.desc: Optional. Resolution of each captured frame in pixels (width x height). Defaults to current screen resolution.

stop_movie.description: Stops recording a movie and deletes the stored frame buffer. Does not save the movie to a file; use export_movie before stopping.

list_movie_frames.description: Returns a list of frame names currently stored in the frame buffer.

add_frame.description: Adds a new frame to the movie frame buffer by capturing the current state of a plot. Requires start_movie to have been called first.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Custom name for the captured frame. Defaults to an auto-generated name.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to capture. Defaults to the current active plot.

export_movie.description: Creates a GIF movie by exporting the recorded frames from the frame buffer. Requires start_movie and add_frame to have been called first.

parameters.name: -filename
parameters.desc: Output file name for the movie. A .gif extension is added automatically if not present.

parameters.name: -frame_duration
parameters.desc: Optional. Duration of each frame in units of 1/100 second. Default: 50 (0.5 seconds per frame).

parameters.name: -frames
parameters.desc: Optional. List of specific frame names to export. If not specified, the entire frame buffer is exported.

parameters.name: -overwrite
parameters.desc: Optional. Overwrites an existing file with the same name if specified.

export_view.description: Exports one or more plots to an image file. If -plots is not specified, exports all visible plots.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the output image file.

parameters.name: -format
parameters.desc: Optional. Output image format. Supported formats: "BMP", "EPS", "JPEG", "JPG", "PNG", "PPM", "TIF", "TIFF", "XBM", "XPM".

parameters.name: -overwrite
parameters.desc: Optional. Overwrites existing files if specified.

parameters.name: -plots
parameters.desc: Optional. List of plot names to export. If not specified, all plots are exported.

parameters.name: -resolution
parameters.desc: Optional. Output image resolution in pixels as width x height. Example: 1920x1080.

add_custom_button.description: Adds a custom button to the Scripts toolbar. Works only in interactive mode. If both -icon and -name are specified, only the icon is displayed.

parameters.name: -file
parameters.desc: Path to a Tcl file to execute when the button is clicked.

parameters.name: -script
parameters.desc: Inline Tcl script to execute when the button is clicked.

parameters.name: -separator
parameters.desc: Adds a visual separator between buttons in the Scripts toolbar.

parameters.name: -desc
parameters.desc: Optional. Tooltip description shown when hovering over the button.

parameters.name: -icon
parameters.desc: Optional. Path to a graphics file (BMP, GIF, PNG, or SVG) used as the button icon.

parameters.name: -label
parameters.desc: Optional. Text label displayed on the button.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Unique name for the button. Defaults to S<number> with auto-incrementing number.

add_custom_button.example: add_custom_button -script "echo Hello World!" -name Greetings -desc "Echoes a Hello World Message."

list_custom_buttons.description: Returns a list of custom buttons and separators that match the given pattern.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern to filter button names. If not specified, returns all custom buttons.

remove_custom_buttons.description: Removes the specified custom buttons from the Scripts toolbar.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of custom button names to remove.

set_vertical_lines_prop.signature: set_vertical_lines_prop <stringValue> [-line_color <#rrggbb>] [-line_style solid | dot | dash | dashdot | dashdotdot] [-line_width <intValue>] [-plot <stringValue>] [-show | -hide]
set_vertical_lines_prop.description: Sets the visual properties of a vertical line on an xy plot. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the vertical line to modify.

parameters.name: -line_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Color of the vertical line in hex format (#rrggbb). Example: #ff0000 for red.

parameters.name: -line_style
parameters.desc: Optional. Line style: "solid" (continuous line), "dot" (dotted line), "dash" (dashed line), "dashdot" (alternating dash and dot), "dashdotdot" (alternating dash and two dots).

parameters.name: -line_width
parameters.desc: Optional. Width of the vertical line in pixels.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot containing the vertical line. Defaults to the selected plot.

parameters.name: -show | -hide
parameters.desc: Optional. "-show" makes the vertical line visible, "-hide" makes it invisible.

get_vertical_lines_prop.description: Returns the specified property of a vertical line on an xy plot. One query property must be specified. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the vertical line to query.

parameters.name: -line_color
parameters.desc: Returns the color of the vertical line.

parameters.name: -line_style
parameters.desc: Returns the style of the vertical line.

parameters.name: -line_width
parameters.desc: Returns the width of the vertical line in pixels.

parameters.name: -show
parameters.desc: Returns whether the vertical line is visible.

parameters.name: -values
parameters.desc: Returns a list of x-values at which the vertical line is positioned.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot containing the vertical line. Defaults to the selected plot.

list_vertical_lines.description: Returns a list of vertical line names matching the given pattern. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern to filter vertical line names. If not specified, returns all vertical lines.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to search. Defaults to the selected plot.

set_tag_prop.description: Displays custom text in a tag box overlay on 2D and 3D plots. The text size adapts to the box size automatically.

parameters.name: -custom_text
parameters.desc: Optional. Text content to be displayed in the tag. Not shown unless -show is also specified.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot where the tag appears. Defaults to the selected plot.

parameters.name: -show | -hide
parameters.desc: Optional. "-show" displays the tag, "-hide" hides it.

create_plot.description: Creates an empty xy plot, or creates a plot from a loaded 1D, 2D, or 3D dataset, or duplicates an existing plot with its properties.

parameters.name: -1d
parameters.desc: Creates an empty xy plot with no data.

parameters.name: -dataset
parameters.desc: Creates a plot from a loaded 1D, 2D, or 3D dataset.

parameters.name: -duplicate
parameters.desc: Replicates the properties of the specified plot into a new plot (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the new plot.

parameters.name: -ref_plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of a reference plot to inherit field and region properties from (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -tdr_state_index
parameters.desc: Optional. Loads only the specified TDR state index from an already loaded dataset; the resulting plot is not a multistate plot (2D and 3D plots only).

list_plots.description: Returns a list of plot names matching the given pattern. If no pattern is specified, all plots are returned.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern to filter plot names.

parameters.name: -dim
parameters.desc: Optional. Dimension of plots to list: 1 (xy), 2, or 3.

parameters.name: -link
parameters.desc: Optional. Returns only linked plots with the specified link ID.

parameters.name: -selected
parameters.desc: Optional. Returns the currently selected plot.

parameters.name: -show | -hide
parameters.desc: Optional. Filters by visibility: -show lists only visible plots, -hide lists only hidden plots.

select_plots.description: Selects the specified plots. Passing an empty list deselects all plots.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of plot names to be selected.

remove_plots.description: Removes the specified plots from the current session.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of plot names to remove.

move_plot.description: Moves the specified plot to a new position, either relative to the current position or to an absolute position.

parameters.name: -position
parameters.desc: The new position of the plot as a list of double values.

parameters.name: -absolute
parameters.desc: Optional. If specified, moves the plot to an absolute position rather than a relative offset.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to be moved. If not specified, uses the current active plot.

zoom_plot.description: Zooms into or out of a plot using various methods: axis range, bounding box, zoom factor, window selection, or reset to original view.

parameters.name: -axis
parameters.desc: Specifies the axis (x, y, or z) to which the range is applied.

parameters.name: -range
parameters.desc: Defines the minimum and maximum values for the specified axis.

parameters.name: -box
parameters.desc: Defines the three ranges for the boundary box as {minX maxX minY maxY minZ maxZ}.

parameters.name: -factor
parameters.desc: Zoom factor. A value greater than 1 zooms in; a value smaller than 1 zooms out.

parameters.name: -reset
parameters.desc: Resets the zoom to the original view.

parameters.name: -window
parameters.desc: Zooms into the specified window between two coordinate pairs. For 3D plots, accepts pixel values (integers) or normalized screen values (doubles between 0 and 1).

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to zoom. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

rotate_plot.description: Rotates a 3D plot by spherical coordinates, directional angle, plane, or individual axis rotation. Multiple axes can be rotated simultaneously. Applies to 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -alpha
parameters.desc: Rotates the plot using the alpha spherical coordinate angle in degrees.

parameters.name: -psi
parameters.desc: Rotates the plot using the psi spherical coordinate angle in degrees.

parameters.name: -theta
parameters.desc: Rotates the plot using the theta spherical coordinate angle in degrees.

parameters.name: -angle
parameters.desc: Rotation angle in degrees, used together with -direction.

parameters.name: -direction
parameters.desc: Direction of rotation: "up" rotates upward, "down" rotates downward, "left" rotates leftward, "right" rotates rightward.

parameters.name: -plane
parameters.desc: Plane of rotation: "xy", "yz", or "xz".

parameters.name: -x
parameters.desc: Rotation angle around the x-axis in degrees.

parameters.name: -y
parameters.desc: Rotation angle around the y-axis in degrees.

parameters.name: -z
parameters.desc: Rotation angle around the z-axis in degrees.

parameters.name: -absolute
parameters.desc: Optional. If specified, rotates to an absolute position rather than a relative rotation.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to rotate. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

link_plots.signature: link_plots <stringList> [-id <intValue>] [-special {axes_prop axis_x axis_y axis_y2 blanking curve_prop cuts deformation field_prop field_sel grid_prop legend_prop matreg move plot_mode plot_prop streamlines}] [-unlink]
link_plots.description: Links plot properties of two or more plots so that changes to one plot are propagated to the others. Specific property categories can be linked selectively using -special.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of all plots to be linked.

parameters.name: -id
parameters.desc: Optional. Integer identifier for the group of linked plots.

parameters.name: -special
parameters.desc: Optional. Links only the specified property categories: "axes_prop" (axes properties, 2D only), "axis_x"/"axis_y"/"axis_y2" (individual axis properties, xy only), "blanking" (blanking operations, 2D/3D), "curve_prop" (curve properties, xy only), "cuts" (cutlines and cutplanes, 2D/3D), "deformation" (deformation operations, 2D/3D), "field_prop" (field range/scale/levels, 2D/3D), "field_sel" (field selection, 2D/3D), "grid_prop" (grid properties, xy only), "legend_prop" (legend properties), "matreg" (material and region properties), "move" (zoom/pan/rotation, 3D only), "plot_mode" (select/zoom/probe mode), "plot_prop" (plot properties except title text), "streamlines" (streamline operations, 2D/3D).

parameters.name: -unlink
parameters.desc: Optional. Removes linking between the specified plots.

diff_plots.description: Creates a new dataset with the difference in the common fields of the specified plots. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of plots to use for creating the differential plot.

parameters.name: -display
parameters.desc: Optional. If specified, creates a new plot with the field difference dataset.

parameters.name: -normalized
parameters.desc: Optional. If specified, normalizes the values between the two plots.

overlay_plots.description: Overlays two or more plots (or datasets) together, creating a new combined plot. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of plots to be overlaid onto the first plot. If only one plot is given, overlays the plot onto the list of datasets specified by -datasets.

parameters.name: -datasets
parameters.desc: Optional. List of datasets to overlay onto the specified plots.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the new overlaid plot. If not specified, a generic name is assigned.

save_plot_to_script.description: Exports the plot properties and curve data of the specified plot to a Tcl script file. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Path (absolute or relative) where the resulting Tcl file will be saved.

parameters.name: -overwrite
parameters.desc: Optional. If specified, overwrites the target Tcl file if it already exists.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to export. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

set_plot_prop.description: Sets various visual properties of a plot, including colors, visibility, aspect ratio, title, TDR state, and transformation. Multiple properties can be set in a single call.

parameters.name: -axes_interchanged | -not_axes_interchanged
parameters.desc: Interchanges or restores the x and y axes (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -bg_gradient | -bg_solid
parameters.desc: Sets the plot background to a gradient or solid color (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -color_bg
parameters.desc: Background color in #rrggbb format, used when a solid background is active.

parameters.name: -color_fg
parameters.desc: Foreground color in #rrggbb format.

parameters.name: -color_map
parameters.desc: Color map mode: "default" uses the full color palette, "grayscale" uses only grayscale colors (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -contacts_color
parameters.desc: Contact color behavior: "constant" (uniform color), "list" (from user preferences), or "map" (from user preferences).

parameters.name: -enable_path_limit | -disable_path_limit
parameters.desc: Activates or deactivates the path limit for the path displayed in the plot title.

parameters.name: -frame_width
parameters.desc: Plot frame width in pixels, must be a positive integer less than 8 (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -gradientColors
parameters.desc: Two background colors for gradient mode as a list, where the first is the top color and the second is the bottom color (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -keep_aspect_ratio | -not_keep_aspect_ratio
parameters.desc: Enables or disables maintaining the aspect ratio (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -materialColors
parameters.desc: Color scheme for materials: "classic" or "vivid" (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -path_depth
parameters.desc: Number of path directory names to display in the plot title (0 to 99, does not include the file name).

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. List of plot names. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

parameters.name: -ratio_xtoy
parameters.desc: Transformation ratio between the x-axis and y-axis (2D plots only).

parameters.name: -reset
parameters.desc: Resets all plot properties to their default values.

parameters.name: -show | -hide
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the entire plot.

parameters.name: -show_axes | -hide_axes
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the axes.

parameters.name: -show_axes_label | -hide_axes_label
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the axes labels (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -show_axes_title | -hide_axes_title
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the axes titles (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -show_cube_axes | -hide_cube_axes
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the cube axes (3D plots only).

parameters.name: -show_curve_lines | -hide_curve_lines
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the curve lines (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -show_curve_markers | -hide_curve_markers
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the curve markers (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -show_grid | -hide_grid
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the grid (xy and 2D plots only).

parameters.name: -show_legend | -hide_legend
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the plot legend.

parameters.name: -show_major_ticks | -hide_major_ticks
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the major ticks (3D plots only).

parameters.name: -show_max_marker | -hide_max_marker
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the maximum value marker (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -show_min_marker | -hide_min_marker
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the minimum value marker (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -show_minor_ticks | -hide_minor_ticks
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the minor ticks (3D plots only).

parameters.name: -show_path | -hide_path
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the file path in the plot title.

parameters.name: -show_regions_bg | -hide_regions_bg
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the background color of regions.

parameters.name: -show_title | -hide_title
parameters.desc: Shows or hides the plot title.

parameters.name: -tdr_state | -tdr_state_index
parameters.desc: Sets the current TDR state by name or by index (2D and 3D plots only). Use -tdr_state_index -1 to display the last state.

parameters.name: -title
parameters.desc: Plot title text.

parameters.name: -title_font_att
parameters.desc: Title font attribute: "normal", "bold", "italic", "underline", or "strikeout".

parameters.name: -title_font_color
parameters.desc: Title font color in #rrggbb format.

parameters.name: -title_font_factor
parameters.desc: Multiplier for the title font size (2D and 3D plots only).

parameters.name: -title_font_family
parameters.desc: Title font family: "arial", "courier", or "times".

parameters.name: -title_font_size
parameters.desc: Title font size in points (xy plots only).

parameters.name: -transformation
parameters.desc: Linear coordinate transformation as {x y z} scaling factors (3D plots only).

get_plot_prop.description: Returns the current value of a single plot property. Only one property can be queried at a time.

parameters.name: -axes_interchanged
parameters.desc: Returns whether the axes are interchanged (2D plots only).

parameters.name: -color_bg
parameters.desc: Returns the background color.

parameters.name: -color_fg
