set_legend_prop.signature: set_legend_prop [-color_bg <#rrggbb>] [-color_fg <#rrggbb>] [-label_font_att normal|bold|italic|underline|strikeout] [-label_font_color <#rrggbb>] [-label_font_factor <doubleValue>] [-label_font_family arial|courier|times] [-label_font_size <intValue>] [-label_format preferred|scientific|engineering|fixed] [-location top_left|top_right|bottom_left|bottom_right] [-margins <intList>] [-nof_labels <intValue>] [-orientation vertical|horizontal] [-plot <stringValue>] [-position {<x> <y>}] [-precision <intValue>] [-reset] [-show_background|-hide_background] [-size {<x> <y>}] [-title_font_att normal|bold|italic|underline|strikeout] [-title_font_color <#rrggbb>] [-title_font_factor <doubleValue>] [-title_font_family arial|courier|times]
set_legend_prop.description: Sets legend properties including position, colors, fonts, orientation, and label formatting. Some properties apply only to xy plots (color_bg, color_fg, label_font_size, location, margins) or 2D/3D plots (label_format, nof_labels, orientation, precision).

parameters.name: -color_bg
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the background color of the legend in #rrggbb hex format. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -color_fg
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the foreground color of the legend in #rrggbb hex format. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -label_font_att
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font attribute of legend labels: normal, bold, italic, underline, or strikeout.

parameters.name: -label_font_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font color of legend labels in #rrggbb hex format.

parameters.name: -label_font_factor
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font size multiplier for legend labels. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -label_font_family
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font family for legend labels: "arial", "courier", or "times".

parameters.name: -label_font_size
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font size of legend labels in points. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -label_format
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the numeric format of legend labels: "preferred", "scientific", "engineering", or "fixed". Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -location
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the legend position in the plot area: "top_left", "top_right", "bottom_left", or "bottom_right". Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -margins
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the legend box margins as a list of integers. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -nof_labels
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the number of labels displayed in the legend. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -orientation
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the legend orientation: "vertical" (labels stacked vertically) or "horizontal" (labels arranged horizontally). Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -position
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the legend position as {x y} normalized to window coordinates (0 to 1).

parameters.name: -precision
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the numeric precision of legend labels. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -reset
parameters.desc: Optional. Resets legend properties to default values.

parameters.name: -show_background|-hide_background
parameters.desc: Optional. "-show_background" sets a solid legend background; "-hide_background" makes the legend background translucent.

parameters.name: -size
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the legend size as {x y} normalized to window coordinates.

parameters.name: -title_font_att
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font attribute of the legend title: normal, bold, italic, underline, or strikeout.

parameters.name: -title_font_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font color of the legend title in #rrggbb hex format.

parameters.name: -title_font_factor
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font size multiplier for the legend title.

parameters.name: -title_font_family
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the font family for the legend title: "arial", "courier", or "times".

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to modify. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

parameters.name: -label_font_color

set_camera_prop.description: Sets camera properties for 3D plots, including position, focal point, projection mode, rotation point, and view angle. Applies to 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -dolly_zoom_on|-dolly_zoom_off
parameters.desc: Optional. "-dolly_zoom_on" enables dolly zoom (zoom by moving camera instead of changing view angle); "-dolly_zoom_off" disables it.

parameters.name: -focal_point
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the focal point of the camera as {x y z} coordinates.

parameters.name: -parallel|-perspective
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the projection mode: "-parallel" for parallel projection (no perspective distortion), "-perspective" for perspective projection (objects appear smaller when farther away).

parameters.name: -position
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the camera position as {x y z} coordinates.

parameters.name: -reset
parameters.desc: Optional. Resets camera settings to default values.

parameters.name: -rot_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the color of the rotation point indicator in #rrggbb hex format.

parameters.name: -rot_size
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the size of the rotation point indicator.

parameters.name: -rot_width
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the width of the rotation point indicator lines.

parameters.name: -rotation_point
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the rotation center of the structure as {x y z} coordinates.

parameters.name: -setup
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the new camera setup as a list of double values.

parameters.name: -show_rotation_point|-hide_rotation_point
parameters.desc: Optional. Shows or hides the rotation point indicator on the plot.

parameters.name: -view_angle
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the camera view angle in degrees.

parameters.name: -view_up
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the view-up vector of the camera as a list of double values.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to modify. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

get_camera_prop.description: Returns a single camera property at a time. Applies to 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -dolly_zoom_on
parameters.desc: Returns whether dolly zoom mode is active.

parameters.name: -focal_point
parameters.desc: Returns the camera focal point position.

parameters.name: -parallel
parameters.desc: Returns whether parallel projection mode is active.

parameters.name: -position
parameters.desc: Returns the camera position.

parameters.name: -rot_color
parameters.desc: Returns the color of the rotation point indicator.

parameters.name: -rot_size
parameters.desc: Returns the size of the rotation point indicator.

parameters.name: -rot_width
parameters.desc: Returns the width of the rotation point indicator lines.

parameters.name: -rotation_point
parameters.desc: Returns the position of the rotation point.

parameters.name: -setup
parameters.desc: Returns the current camera setup.

parameters.name: -show_rotation_point

parameters.name: -view_angle
parameters.desc: Returns the camera view angle in degrees.

parameters.name: -view_up
parameters.desc: Returns the view-up vector of the camera.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

set_ruler_prop.description: Sets ruler properties including color, endpoints, precision, label visibility, and snap-to-mesh behavior. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the color of the ruler in #rrggbb hex format.

parameters.name: -id
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the ID of the ruler to modify. If not specified, uses the last selected ruler.

parameters.name: -pos1
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the position of the first endpoint of the ruler.

parameters.name: -pos2
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the position of the second endpoint of the ruler.

parameters.name: -precision
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the decimal precision of the ruler measurements.

parameters.name: -show_label|-hide_label
parameters.desc: Optional. Shows or hides the ruler distance label.

parameters.name: -snap_on|-snap_off
parameters.desc: Optional. "-snap_on" activates snap-to-mesh (ruler endpoints snap to the nearest mesh nodes); "-snap_off" disables it.

parameters.name: -width
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the ruler line width in pixels.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot to modify. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

get_ruler_prop.description: Returns a single ruler property at a time. The command returns only one property per call.

parameters.name: -color
parameters.desc: Returns the color of the ruler.

parameters.name: -pos1
parameters.desc: Returns the position of the first endpoint of the ruler.

parameters.name: -pos2

parameters.name: -precision
parameters.desc: Returns the decimal precision of the ruler measurements.

parameters.name: -show_label

parameters.name: -snap_on

parameters.name: -width

set_band_diagram.description: Creates a band diagram from plots created with the cutline function. The input is a list of plot names whose data is used to compose the band diagram.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: Optional. List of plot names created with the cutline function. If not specified, uses the selected plots.

create_streamline.description: Creates a new streamline on a 2D or 3D plot by tracing the path of a vector field from a seed point or between two points. Uses the Runge-Kutta 4 algorithm for integration. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -direction
parameters.desc: Required. Direction of the streamline: "forward" (from seed point following the field), "backward" (against the field), or "both" (bidirectional). Default is "both".

parameters.name: -field
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the vector field on which to create the streamline.

parameters.name: -point
parameters.desc: Seed point as {x y [z]}. For "forward" direction, this is the starting point; for "backward", the end point; for "both", the middle point. Mutually exclusive with -p1/-p2.

parameters.name: -p1
parameters.desc: First endpoint as {x y [z]} for creating multiple streamlines between two points. Must be used with -p2 and -nofpoints.

parameters.name: -p2
parameters.desc: Second endpoint as {x y [z]} for creating multiple streamlines between two points. Must be used with -p1 and -nofpoints.

parameters.name: -nofpoints
parameters.desc: Number of streamlines to create between -p1 and -p2. For example, 9 creates 7 additional streamlines plus the two endpoint streamlines.

parameters.name: -geom
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the geometry in the plot. If not specified, uses the first geometry associated with the plot.

parameters.name: -integ_initial_step
parameters.desc: Optional. Initial step size used in the Runge-Kutta 4 algorithm. This step remains constant throughout integration.

parameters.name: -integ_max_propagation
parameters.desc: Optional. Maximum total length of the streamline. This limits the amount of data calculated. The -line_resolution value of set_streamline_prop must not be less than this value.

parameters.name: -integ_max_steps
parameters.desc: Optional. Maximum number of iterations of the Runge-Kutta 4 algorithm.

parameters.name: -integ_terminal_speed
parameters.desc: Optional. Terminal speed threshold; if the next iteration result is lower than this value, integration stops. This prevents streamline tracing in near-zero field regions.

parameters.name: -materials
parameters.desc: Optional. List of materials on which the streamline will be created. Default is all materials. Mutually exclusive with -regions.

parameters.name: -regions
parameters.desc: Optional. List of regions on which the streamline will be created. Default is all regions. Mutually exclusive with -materials.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the new streamline. If not specified, a default name is generated.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

extract_streamlines.description: Extracts the field and coordinate data from one or more streamlines created in 2D or 3D plots, returning 1D datasets. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of streamline names from which to extract data.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

list_streamlines.description: Returns a list of streamline names created on the specified plot. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern to filter streamline names. If not specified, all streamlines are returned.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

remove_streamlines.description: Removes the specified streamlines from a plot.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of streamline names to remove.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot from which to remove streamlines. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

set_streamline_prop.description: Sets the visual properties of streamlines, including line style, arrow appearance, and direction. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: List of streamline names to modify.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

parameters.name: -arrow_angle
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the arrowhead angle in degrees. Must be between 1 and 89.

parameters.name: -arrow_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the color of the arrows in #rrggbb hex format.

parameters.name: -arrow_size
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the size of the arrows.

parameters.name: -arrow_step
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the step distance between consecutive arrows. This value must be greater than the line resolution.

parameters.name: -arrow_style
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the arrow line style: "solid", "dash", "dot", "dashdot", or "dashdotdot".

parameters.name: -arrow_width
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the width of the arrowheads.

parameters.name: -constant_arrow|-normal_arrow
parameters.desc: Optional. "-constant_arrow" keeps arrowhead size constant on screen regardless of zoom level; "-normal_arrow" makes arrowheads scale with zoom level.

parameters.name: -line_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the streamline line color in #rrggbb hex format.

parameters.name: -line_resolution
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the distance between points composing the line. Lower values give smoother lines but reduce performance.

parameters.name: -line_style
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the line style: "solid", "dash", "dot", "dashdot", or "dashdotdot".

parameters.name: -line_width
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the line width in pixels.

parameters.name: -positive_direction|-negative_direction
parameters.desc: Optional. "-positive_direction" shows arrows in the normal flow direction; "-negative_direction" inverts the arrows. Default is "-positive_direction".

parameters.name: -show_arrows|-hide_arrows
parameters.desc: Optional. Shows or hides the arrows on the streamline.

parameters.name: -show_line|-hide_line
parameters.desc: Optional. Shows or hides the streamline line.

get_streamline_prop.description: Returns a single property of a streamline. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the streamline from which to query the property.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

parameters.name: -arrow_angle
parameters.desc: Returns the arrowhead angle in degrees.

parameters.name: -arrow_color
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow color.

parameters.name: -arrow_size
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow size.

parameters.name: -arrow_step
parameters.desc: Returns the step distance between arrows.

parameters.name: -arrow_style
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow line style.

parameters.name: -arrow_width
parameters.desc: Returns the arrowhead width.

parameters.name: -constant_arrow
parameters.desc: Returns whether constant-size arrowheads are used regardless of zoom level.

parameters.name: -line_color
parameters.desc: Returns the streamline line color.

parameters.name: -line_resolution
parameters.desc: Returns the distance between line points.

parameters.name: -line_style
parameters.desc: Returns the line style.

parameters.name: -line_width
parameters.desc: Returns the line width.

parameters.name: -positive_direction
parameters.desc: Returns whether arrows are shown in normal or inverted direction.

parameters.name: -show_arrows
parameters.desc: Returns whether arrows are visible.

parameters.name: -show_line
parameters.desc: Returns whether the streamline line is visible.

set_vector_prop.description: Sets the visual properties of a vector field, including arrow shape, size, color, scale mode, and visibility. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the vector field to modify.

parameters.name: -constant_heads|-normal_heads
parameters.desc: Optional. "-constant_heads" makes all arrows the same screen size regardless of vector magnitude; "-normal_heads" makes arrow size proportional to vector magnitude.

parameters.name: -fill_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the fill color of solid arrowheads in #rrggbb hex format. Only effective with solid arrowhead shapes.

parameters.name: -geom
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the geometry where the vector field is located.

parameters.name: -head_angle
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the arrowhead angle in degrees. Must be between 1 and 89.

parameters.name: -head_shape
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the arrow shape: "arrow" (outlined arrow), "arrow_solid" (filled arrow), "head" (outlined arrowhead), "head_closed" (closed outlined arrowhead), or "head_solid" (filled arrowhead).

parameters.name: -head_size
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the length of the arrows.

parameters.name: -line_color
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the color of the arrows in #rrggbb hex format.

parameters.name: -line_pattern
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the line pattern of the arrows: "solid", "dot", "dash", "dashdot", or "dashdotdot".

parameters.name: -line_width
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the width of the arrows in pixels.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

parameters.name: -scale
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the scale mode for displaying arrows: "grid" (arrows placed on a regular grid) or "uniform" (arrows placed at mesh nodes with uniform scaling).

parameters.name: -scale_factor_grid
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the grid density factor for arrow placement when using "-scale grid".

parameters.name: -scale_factor_uniform
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the uniform scaling factor for arrow size when using "-scale uniform".

parameters.name: -show|-hide
parameters.desc: Optional. Shows or hides the vector field arrows.

get_vector_prop.description: Returns a single property of a vector field. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the vector field to query.

parameters.name: -constant_heads
parameters.desc: Returns whether constant-size arrowheads are used regardless of vector magnitude.

parameters.name: -fill_color
parameters.desc: Returns the fill color of solid arrowheads.

parameters.name: -head_angle
parameters.desc: Returns the arrowhead angle in degrees.

parameters.name: -head_shape
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow shape.

parameters.name: -head_size
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow size.

parameters.name: -line_color
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow line color.

parameters.name: -line_pattern
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow line pattern.

parameters.name: -line_width
parameters.desc: Returns the arrow line width.

parameters.name: -scale
parameters.desc: Returns the scale mode: "uniform" or "grid".

parameters.name: -scale_factor_grid
parameters.desc: Returns the grid factor for arrow placement.

parameters.name: -scale_factor_uniform
parameters.desc: Returns the uniform scaling factor for arrow size.

parameters.name: -show
parameters.desc: Returns whether the vector field arrows are visible.

parameters.name: -geom
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the geometry where the vector field is located.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

calculate.description: Extracts FET electrical parameters (threshold voltage, transconductance, saturation current, off-current, output resistance, on-resistance) from Id-Vd or Id-Vg curves. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Name of the curve on which to apply the parameter extraction.

parameters.name: -op
parameters.desc: Required. Parameter to extract: "vth" (threshold voltage), "gmmax" (maximum transconductance), "idsat" (saturation drain current), "ioff" (off-state current), "rout" (output resistance), or "ron" (on-resistance).

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot containing the curve. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

calculate_scalar.description: Calculates a scalar value by evaluating a mathematical formula over curves or variables. The last operation in the formula must return a scalar (double) value. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -curves|-variables
parameters.desc: Required. "-curves" evaluates the formula over curve data; "-variables" evaluates over variable data.

parameters.name: -function
parameters.desc: Required. Mathematical formula to evaluate. Must use scalar-returning functions (see Appendix D). Curve names are enclosed in angle brackets, for example: vecmax(<Curve_1>) + vecmin(<Curve_1>).

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot containing the curves. If not specified, uses the selected xy plot. Only applicable with -curves.

calculate_field_value.description: Calculates the minimum or maximum value of a scalar field and shows the location with a marker. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -min|-max
parameters.desc: Required. "-min" finds the minimum field value; "-max" finds the maximum field value.

parameters.name: -dataset
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects the dataset from which to extract data (for batch mode only). Mutually exclusive with -plot.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects the plot from which to extract data. Mutually exclusive with -dataset.

parameters.name: -field
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the scalar field. If not specified, uses the current contour-band field displayed in the plot.

parameters.name: -geom
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects the geometry from which to extract data.

parameters.name: -materials
parameters.desc: Optional. List of materials to restrict the search. Mutually exclusive with -regions.

parameters.name: -regions
parameters.desc: Optional. List of regions to restrict the search. Mutually exclusive with -materials.

parameters.name: -ranges
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies a bounding box {x1 x2 y1 y2 [z1 z2]} to restrict the search area.

find_values.description: Returns the positions of all points in a scalar field that match the specified value. Applies to 2D and 3D plots only.

parameters.name: <doubleValue>
parameters.desc: Required. The value to search for in the field (floating-point number).

parameters.name: -field
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the scalar field to search.

parameters.name: -geom
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the dataset or geometry to search. If not specified, uses the main one from the active plot.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

parameters.name: -window
parameters.desc: Optional. Restricts the search to a Cartesian window defined as {x1 y1 [z1] x2 y2 [z2]}. If not specified, uses the entire geometry.

get_variable_data.description: Returns a list of values for the specified variable from the given dataset. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the variable whose data is to be retrieved.

parameters.name: -dataset
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the dataset containing the variable.

list_variables.description: Returns a list of variable names matching the given search pattern from the specified dataset. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -dataset
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the dataset to list variables from.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Optional. Search pattern to filter variable names. If not specified, all variable names are returned.

create_variable.description: Creates a new variable in a dataset, either from a list of explicit values or by evaluating a mathematical function. Applies to xy plots only.

parameters.name: -dataset
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the dataset where the new variable will be added.

parameters.name: -function
parameters.desc: Expression to evaluate to generate variable values. Mutually exclusive with -values.

parameters.name: -values
parameters.desc: List of explicit double values to assign to the new variable. Mutually exclusive with -function.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Required. Name for the new variable.

export_variables.description: Exports variables from a dataset to a CSV file. If no variable list is specified, all variables in the dataset are exported.

parameters.name: -dataset
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the dataset from which to export variables.

parameters.name: -filename
parameters.desc: Required. Path of the output file.

parameters.name: -overwrite
parameters.desc: Optional. If specified, overwrites an existing file without prompting.

parameters.name: <stringList>
parameters.desc: Optional. List of variable names to export. If not specified, exports all variables from the dataset.

extract_path.description: Extracts the path of minimum or maximum values of a scalar field, returning a new geometry added to the plot. For 2D plots, uses a polygon extrusion approach; for 3D plots, can specify start and end points directly.

parameters.name: <stringValue>
parameters.desc: Required. Name of the scalar field whose path is extracted.

parameters.name: -max|-min
parameters.desc: Required. "-max" extracts the path of maximum values; "-min" extracts the path of minimum values.

parameters.name: -bottom
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the coordinate of the bottom face of the extruded polygon along the axis specified by -type.

parameters.name: -geom
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the dataset or geometry to search. If not specified, uses the main one from the active plot.

parameters.name: -materials
parameters.desc: Optional. List of materials where the path will be extracted. Mutually exclusive with -regions.

parameters.name: -regions
parameters.desc: Optional. List of regions where the path will be extracted. Mutually exclusive with -materials.

parameters.name: -plot
parameters.desc: Optional. Name of the plot. If not specified, uses the selected plot.

parameters.name: -points
parameters.desc: Optional. Coordinates of polygon vertices for extrusion. Points must be ordered as they will be interpolated along the polygon edges.

parameters.name: -start
parameters.desc: Optional. Start point of the 3D path as {x y z}. Available for 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -end
parameters.desc: Optional. End point of the 3D path as {x y z}. Available for 3D plots only.

parameters.name: -top
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets the coordinate of the top face of the extruded polygon along the axis specified by -type.

parameters.name: -type
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects the extrusion axis for the polygon: "x", "y", or "z".

parameters.name: -window
parameters.desc: Optional. Restricts the search to a Cartesian window {x1 y1 [z1] x2 y2 [z2]}.

ext::AbsList.description: Computes the absolute value of all elements of a list and stores the results in a named list.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Name of a list to store the list of absolute values.

parameters.name: -x
parameters.desc: Input list of real numbers.

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level: 0 (warnings/errors only), 1 (echo results), 2 (progress and debug), 3 (all debug). Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::DiffForwardList.description: Computes the first-order derivative of a curve using the forward finite difference method. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of x-values and y-values. Results are stored in an array with elements 'x' (midpoint x-values) and 'dy' (derivative values).

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Name of an array to store results. The array has string-valued indices 'x' and 'dy'.

parameters.name: -x
parameters.desc: List of x-values (independent variable).

parameters.name: -y
parameters.desc: List of y-values (dependent variable).

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level: 0 (warnings/errors only), 1 (echo results), 2 (progress and debug), 3 (all debug). Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::DiffList.description: Computes the first-order derivative of a curve using the central finite difference method with linear or logarithmic interpolation. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of x-values and y-values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Name of a list to store the first-order derivative values.

parameters.name: -x
parameters.desc: List of x-values (independent variable).

parameters.name: -y
parameters.desc: List of y-values (dependent variable).

parameters.name: -yLog
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects linear (0) or logarithmic (1) interpolation for y-axis values when computing the derivative. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -xLog
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects linear (0) or logarithmic (1) interpolation for x-axis values when computing the derivative. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level: 0 (warnings/errors only), 1 (echo results), 2 (progress and debug), 3 (all debug). Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractBVi.description: Extracts the breakdown voltage from an I-V curve, defined as the bias voltage at which the current reaches a specified level. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the breakdown voltage value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of current values.

parameters.name: -io
parameters.desc: Current level threshold (nonzero real number) at which breakdown voltage is defined.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "BVi".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3e".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractBVv.description: Extracts the breakdown voltage from an I-V curve, defined as the maximum voltage that can be applied to a contact. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the breakdown voltage value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of current values.

parameters.name: -sign
parameters.desc: Distinguishes transistor type: +1 for n-p-n transistor, -1 for p-n-p transistor. In general, set to -1 if breakdown occurs at negative bias.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "BVv".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.2e".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractEarlyV.description: Extracts the Early voltage from an Ic-Vce curve. The Early voltage is determined from the slope of the Ic-Vce curve at a specified bias point. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the Early voltage value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of collector-emitter voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of collector current values.

parameters.name: -vo
parameters.desc: Bias point at which the slope of the Ic-Vce curve is determined for the Early voltage computation.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "Va".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3e".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractExtremum.description: Extracts the maximum or minimum value of a curve. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of x-values and y-values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the extremum value.

parameters.name: -x
parameters.desc: List of x-values.

parameters.name: -y
parameters.desc: List of y-values.

parameters.name: -type
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects whether to extract the minimum ("min") or maximum ("max") of the curve. Default: "max".

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "out".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3e".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractGm.description: Extracts the maximum transconductance (gm = dId/dVg) from an Id-Vgs curve. The gate bias at which the maximum gm occurs is computed using parabolic interpolation. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the maximum transconductance value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of gate voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of drain current values.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "gm".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3e".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractIoff.description: Extracts the drain leakage current at a specified gate voltage from an Id-Vgs curve (computed for a high drain bias). A small nonzero gate voltage is recommended to avoid noise, such as 0.1 mV for NMOS or -0.1 mV for PMOS.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the drain leakage current value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of gate voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of drain current values.

parameters.name: -vo
parameters.desc: Gate voltage at which the drain leakage current is extracted. Use a small but nonzero value.

parameters.name: -log10
parameters.desc: Optional. If set to 1, returns log10(Ioff) instead of Ioff. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "Ioff".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3e".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractRdiff.description: Extracts the differential resistance (Rdiff = dV/dI) from an I-V curve at a specified voltage. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the differential resistance value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of current values.

parameters.name: -vo
parameters.desc: Voltage at which the differential resistance is extracted.

parameters.name: -yLog
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects linear (0) or logarithmic (1) interpolation for y-axis values when computing the derivative. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -xLog
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects linear (0) or logarithmic (1) interpolation for x-axis values when computing the derivative. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "Rdiff".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3f".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractRsh.description: Calculates the sheet resistance (Ohm/sq) and p-n junction depth of semiconductor layers in the vertical direction in a 2D structure by creating an axis-aligned cutline. Also computes the total sheet resistance (sum of each layer). Applies only to 2D structures.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Name of an array to store results. Contains elements: d (thickness per layer), Rsh (sheet resistance per layer), RshTotal (total sheet resistance), RshTop (top layer sheet resistance), xjTop (junction depth), X/Y (position values), DopingConcentration, eMobility, hMobility, eDensity, hDensity, Conductivity, and Resistivity.

parameters.name: -dataset
parameters.desc: Name of the dataset from which the outline will be generated.

parameters.name: -semdataset
parameters.desc: Name of the dataset containing the variables X/Y, DopingConcentration, eMobility, hMobility, eDensity, hDensity, Conductivity, and Resistivity.

parameters.name: -type
parameters.desc: Links the outline to the specified axis: "x" links to x-axis and creates outline aligned to y-axis (vertical), "y" links to y-axis and creates outline aligned to x-axis (vertical).

parameters.name: -at
parameters.desc: Value where the axis-aligned outline cuts the linked axis.

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractSS.description: Extracts the subthreshold voltage swing (SS) in mV/decade at a specified gate voltage from an Id-Vgs curve. SS is defined as 1000 / (d/dVg * log10(Id)). Better results are obtained when the specified gate voltage is well below threshold and is a small nonzero value.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the subthreshold swing value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of gate voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of absolute drain current values.

parameters.name: -vo
parameters.desc: Gate voltage at which the slope is extracted. Should be a value well below the threshold voltage.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "SS".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3f".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractSsub.description: Extracts the subthreshold voltage swing (SS) in mV/decade from an Id-Vgs curve. SS is defined as 1000 / Max(d/dVg * log10(Id)), where the maximum slope is found automatically. Unlike ext::ExtractSS, no specific gate voltage needs to be specified.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the subthreshold swing value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of gate voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of absolute drain current values.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "ss".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3f".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractValue.description: For a given target x-value, extracts the n-th interpolated y-value from a curve. To find the interpolated x-value for a given y-value, swap the -x and -y arguments. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of x-values and y-values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the interpolated y-value.

parameters.name: -x
parameters.desc: List of x-values.

parameters.name: -y
parameters.desc: List of y-values.

parameters.name: -xo
parameters.desc: Target x-value for interpolation.

parameters.name: -occurrence
parameters.desc: Optional. Specifies the n-th interpolated y-value to extract (1-based index). Default: 1.

parameters.name: -yLog
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects linear (0) or logarithmic (1) interpolation for y-axis values. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -xLog
parameters.desc: Optional. Selects linear (0) or logarithmic (1) interpolation for x-axis values. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "SS".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3f".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractVdlin.description: Extracts the drain voltage for a given drain current level from an Id-Vds curve using linear interpolation. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the drain voltage value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of drain voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of drain current values.

parameters.name: -io
parameters.desc: Target drain current level.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "ss".

parameters.name: -f
parameters.desc: Optional. Format string for the extracted value written to the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Default: "%.3f".

parameters.name: -info
parameters.desc: Optional. Sets local information level. Default: 0.

parameters.name: -help
parameters.desc: Optional. Prints a help screen if set to 1. Default: 0.

ext::ExtractVdlog.description: Extracts the drain voltage for a given drain current level from an Id-Vds curve using logarithmic interpolation. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.

parameters.name: -out
parameters.desc: Variable name to store the drain voltage value.

parameters.name: -v
parameters.desc: List of drain voltage values.

parameters.name: -i
parameters.desc: List of drain current values.

parameters.name: -io
parameters.desc: Target drain current level.

parameters.name: -name
parameters.desc: Optional. Name for the extracted variable in the Sentaurus Workbench Family Tree. Use "noprint" to suppress extraction, "out" to use the -out variable name. Default: "SS".

parameters.name: -info

ext::ExtractVglin.description: Extracts the gate voltage for a given drain current level from an Id-Vgs curve using linear interpolation. The curve is represented by two Tcl lists of voltage and current values.
